title: Backend version log
summary: Adds a protected Ops backend page that lists implemented features and fixes by version.
changes: Expose a readable version history in the Ops backend.
Use structured release entries with version, date, category, summary and affected areas.
Keep the first documented baseline below v1.0 while pre-exposure work is still active.
security_notes: The version log is only reachable through the protected Ops backend surface.

title: Credential campaign aggregation
summary: Aggregates service-login brute-force campaigns and stores All-Time and per-campaign credential counters.
changes: Sample the first service-login failures and aggregate later attempts into campaign summaries.
Store All-Time Top 100 usernames and cleartext passwords in the protected backend.
Support full CSV export for aggregated username and password counters.
Cap unique All-Time passwords at one million while continuing to count known values.
security_notes: Cleartext attempted passwords are kept out of normal auth events and stored only in dedicated credential tables.
Credential export remains an Ops backend function and must not be exposed on the public honeypot port.

title: Interactive inverter disconnects
summary: Adds visible QDC and QAC controls for inverter and PV field disconnect workflows.
changes: Expose disconnect switches in the single-line view.
Reflect disconnect state in simulated energy output.
Map related control functions through Modbus registers.
security_notes: Unauthenticated switch clicks are logged and redirected to the service-login flow.

title: Single-line energy-flow view
summary: Introduces a clearer interactive single-line diagram for park topology and energy flow.
changes: Separate DC-side PV field paths from AC-side inverter output paths.
Use established electrical symbols for inverter, breaker and grid presentation.
Relax diagram layout to avoid crowded labels and oversized arrows.
security_notes: Control affordances are visible to attackers, while real actions still require the service path.

title: Historical generation simulation
summary: Seeds plausible 30-day generation history so a fresh honeypot looks operational.
changes: Generate minute-level plant output from solar position and weather inputs.
Use historical weather or plausible fallback cloud-cover patterns.
Expose production trends and daily energy bars in the HMI.
security_notes: Historical data is synthetic and does not reveal real plant telemetry.

title: Ops settings and source enrichment
summary: Moves backend runtime settings into the protected Ops UI and enriches source IP overview rows.
changes: Persist backend settings in SQLite instead of growing the environment file.
Add toggles for source IP country, rDNS and ISP enrichment.
Add a protected action for deleting synthetic plant history.
security_notes: rDNS remains opt-in because it creates outbound DNS activity.

title: Dedicated Ops backend
summary: Adds an internal backend surface for observing attacker activity separately from the honeypot UI.
changes: Run the Ops backend on a separate port from the exposed HMI honeypot.
Show events, alerts, sources and runtime summary views.
Keep the backend suitable for VPN, tunnel or private-network access.
security_notes: The Ops backend is a sensitive observer surface and should not share the public honeypot ingress.
